findings.message: archive_item is a new untraced business operation with no structured log on any code path. Auto-instrumentation (FastAPIInstrumentor) creates an HTTP span, but no item_id, outcome, or operation label is recorded in structured fields. A business-level metric (e.g. items_archived_total) is also absent, so archive operations are invisible in dashboards beyond the generic request counter.
findings.suggestion: Import get_logger from demo.logging_config and emit get_logger().info('item_archived', item_id=item_id) before the return. Add a counter increment via request.app.state.metrics or a module-level counter so archive volume is independently observable.

findings.message: 404 error path raises HTTPException with no structured log. The item_id and correlation_id are not captured before raising, so the only signal is an HTTP 404 in the access log — there is no correlated log entry carrying item_id for post-hoc trace lookup.
findings.suggestion: Before raising, call get_logger().warning('item_not_found', item_id=item_id). The structlog context configured in logging_config.py injects the correlation_id automatically, producing a fully correlated log entry that links to the trace span.

findings.message: session.commit() is unguarded: a DB error (IntegrityError, OperationalError, DisconnectedError) propagates to the global exception handler with no log entry capturing item_id or operation context. The auto-instrumented SQLAlchemy span records the SQL error, but the FastAPI span above it carries no item_id or business context, making DB-level failures opaque.
findings.suggestion: Wrap in try/except SQLAlchemyError, emit get_logger().error('item_archive_failed', item_id=item_id, exc_info=True) and re-raise as HTTPException(500) so the structured log, the OTel span error flag, and the HTTP error counter all fire together.

findings.message: Deferred 'from fastapi import HTTPException' inside the function body delays any import-time OTel instrumentation hooks attached to the exception class and silences import errors until the 404 branch is first exercised in production.
findings.suggestion: Hoist the import to the module-level block alongside the existing 'from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, Query'.

findings.message: No SLO threshold is defined for the new write endpoint. The existing SLOs in slo.py (request_latency_p99_ms, error_rate_pct) are global and will have their p99 skewed upward by the DB-write latency of this endpoint, potentially masking latency regressions on the read-only list endpoint — or triggering spurious SLO breaches as archive traffic grows.
findings.suggestion: Either add a per-operation SLO key (e.g. archive_latency_p99_ms) to slo.py/settings.py, or document explicitly that the global budget intentionally covers writes and re-derive the threshold with representative write-path load.

findings.message: Re-archive path is silently accepted: if item.name already starts with '[archived]', the prefix is prepended again with no log or metric, making repeated calls invisible in observability data and producing corrupted names with no alert.
findings.suggestion: Check 'if item.name.startswith("[archived]")' and emit get_logger().warning('item_already_archived', item_id=item_id) (returning early or raising a 409), so duplicate archive calls appear as anomalies in structured logs and can be tracked as a counter.
